hypothesis: A meta-learning approach that adaptively tunes NaviDiv's diversity-constraint thresholds (e.g., fragment size, similarity cutoff, and SMILES 10-gram limits) online during molecular generation will yield superior preservation of multi-metric chemical diversity and equal or better property optimization across diverse tasks (e.g., singlet fission materials, drug-like libraries, and catalytic ligands) and architectures, compared to fixed per-task thresholds.
